story.iWant: to download the current slide as a PNG image
story.soThat: I can share it in documents or emails

subtasks.subtask: Add to script section before application code
Return canvas element or PNG blob
Generate filename with deck name and slide number
Trigger download via hidden anchor click
Connect "Download Current Slide" menu item to function

criterion.given: I am viewing a slide in presentation mode
criterion.when: I click "Download Current Slide" from the download menu

criterion.description: The PNG dimensions are exactly 1920x1080 pixels
The PNG accurately captures all slide content (text, SVGs, backgrounds, gradients)
This works with file:// protocol (opening index.html directly)

doc.title: Technical Specification - Slide Download & Export
doc.snippet: Complete configuration for html2canvas with options: width/height 1920x1080, scale 1, useCORS true, allowTaint true, backgroundColor null. Includes filename convention {deckName}-slide-{N}.png and download trigger pattern using object URLs.

doc.title: Technical Specification - Implementation Guide
doc.snippet: Story 2 implementation guide: 1) Inline html2canvas library in script section, 2) Implement captureSlide(slideNumber) helper, 3) Implement downloadCurrentSlide() function, 4) Wire up menu item click handler, 5) Test with various slide types.

doc.title: Technical Specification - Development Context
doc.snippet: Code style uses camelCase functions, document.getElementById() for DOM queries, inline onclick handlers. CSS patterns include .nav-btn styling, button hover states with border-color transitions. Libraries should be added before application code.

doc.title: Epic 12 Stories
doc.snippet: Depends on Story 12.1 (Download Menu UI). Requires html2canvas integration. Implements captureSlide helper and downloadCurrentSlide function. Access slide via iframe.contentDocument.querySelector('.slide').

file.reason: Placeholder function to replace with actual implementation. Currently shows alert message.
Helper function to close download menu after selection - already implemented.
Menu toggle function from Story 12.1 - already implemented.
Download menu UI with three menu items including "Download Current Slide" button - already implemented from Story 12.1.
Global state variables needed for generating filename and accessing current slide.
Constants for 1920x1080 slide dimensions - reuse for html2canvas configuration.
Iframe element containing current slide - access via contentDocument to get slide element for capture.

package.name: html2canvas
package.purpose: HTML to canvas rendering for PNG capture
package.note: Must be inlined in script section for file:// protocol support

package.name: puppeteer
package.purpose: Headless browser - not used for this story

package.name: googleapis
package.purpose: Google API - not used for this story

constraint.description: Must work with file:// protocol - user opens index.html directly without server
constraint.implication: All libraries must be inlined, no fetch() calls for library loading

constraint.description: All code goes in viewer-template.html - no external JS files
constraint.implication: Inline html2canvas library in script section before application code

constraint.description: Slide content is in iframe from same origin (slides/slide-N.html)
constraint.implication: contentDocument access works for file:// protocol since same origin
Configure html2canvas with explicit width/height, scale: 1
Use camelCase, document.getElementById(), inline onclick handlers

interface.name: html2canvas
URL.createObjectURL
interface.usage: Used to create downloadable URL from PNG blob

interface.name: URL.revokeObjectURL
interface.usage: Clean up object URL after download to prevent memory leaks

interface.name: iframe.contentDocument

tests.standards: No automated testing framework exists. All testing is manual browser testing. Test in Chrome (primary), Safari, and Firefox. Test both file:// protocol (opening index.html directly) and http:// protocol (python3 -m http.server). Verify PNG dimensions using image viewer or properties dialog.

locations.location: Browser DevTools console for error monitoring

ideas.idea: Open viewer in presentation mode, click Download Current Slide, verify download triggers
Open PNG in image viewer, verify dimensions are exactly 1920x1080
Compare PNG to original slide - text, SVGs, backgrounds, gradients should all render correctly
Open index.html via file:// (double-click file), test download works without server
Test with different slide types: title slide, list slide, code slide, diagram slide
Test that menu closes after clicking download option
